blocks.description: Program header (enterprise data governance and quality framework v3.1.0, data steward M. Chen CDMP, domain Enterprise Data Quality Management, revision 3.1.0 with cross-source consistency and timeliness checks), Section 1 header; LIBNAME for source (/data/enterprise/source readonly), dq (/data/governance/quality), meta (/data/governance/metadata), report (/data/governance/reports); OPTIONS MPRINT MLOGIC SYMBOLGEN COMPRESS=YES FULLSTIMER NOFMTERR MINOPERATOR; %LET for completeness_threshold=95, validity_threshold=98, consistency_threshold=99, freshness_max_days=3, run_date via %SYSFUNC(TODAY()), run_datetime via %SYSFUNC(DATETIME()), dq_version=3.1.0, steward_name=M. Chen, domain_name=Enterprise Data Quality; scorecard dimension weights: weight_completeness=0.30, weight_validity=0.30, weight_consistency=0.25, weight_timeliness=0.15

%MACRO profile_table(lib=, table=) opening: Section 2 header (automated data profiling — captures metadata variable names/types/lengths, row counts, distinct counts per column, numeric distributions via PROC UNIVARIATE, categorical frequency analysis via PROC FREQ, compiled into standardized profile dataset); %PUT NOTE with lib and table parameters and TIME() timestamp
PROC CONTENTS inside profile_table: Step 2a — captures variable metadata from &lib..&table into work.contents_&table keeping NAME TYPE LENGTH FORMAT LABEL NOBS; NOPRINT; RUN

PROC SQL NOPRINT inside profile_table: Step 2b — SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :total_rows TRIMMED for total row count; SELECT NAME INTO :col_list SEPARATED BY ' ' FROM work.contents_&table for column name list; %LET n_cols = &SQLOBS to capture column count; QUIT

%DO i = 1 %TO &n_cols inside profile_table: loop over each column to compute distinct value counts; %LET col_name = %SCAN(&col_list, &i); each iteration runs PROC SQL NOPRINT SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT &col_name) INTO :distinct_&col_name TRIMMED; %END

PROC UNIVARIATE inside profile_table: Step 2c — numeric distribution statistics from &lib..&table; NOPRINT; VAR _NUMERIC_; OUTPUT OUT=work.univar_&table with N, NMISS, MEAN, STD, MIN, MAX, MEDIAN, Q1, Q3, P1, P99; RUN
PROC FREQ inside profile_table: Step 2d — categorical frequency analysis from &lib..&table; NOPRINT; TABLES _CHARACTER_ / OUT=work.freq_&table MISSING NOCUM; RUN

DATA meta.profile_&table inside profile_table: Step 2e — compiles profile into standardized format; LENGTH table_name $32 column_name $32 column_type $4 total_rows distinct_count null_count profile_date profiled_by $20; FORMAT profile_date DATE9.; SET work.contents_&table; assigns table_name, profile_date=TODAY(), profiled_by=steward_name; maps TYPE 1=NUM else CHAR; assigns total_rows from macro var; column_name=NAME; distinct_count via SYMGET('distinct_' || STRIP(NAME)); null_count = total_rows - distinct_count; OUTPUT; RUN; %PUT NOTE profiling completed with column and row counts; %MEND profile_table

%MACRO check_completeness(lib=, table=, required_cols=) opening: Section 3 header (validates required columns for null/blank values, computes completeness percentage per column, flags failures against configurable threshold using %DO loop over required columns and PROC SQL); %PUT NOTE with lib, table, required_cols parameters; %LET n_req = %SYSFUNC(COUNTW(&required_cols)) to count required columns

DATA work.completeness_&table inside check_completeness: initializes empty results table with LENGTH table_name $32 column_name $32 total_rows non_null_count completeness_pct threshold check_status $4 check_date; FORMAT completeness_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.; STOP; RUN

%DO j = 1 %TO &n_req inside check_completeness: loop over each required column; %LET req_col = %SCAN; PROC SQL NOPRINT for total_count and non_null_ct (WHERE NOT NULL AND NE ''); DATA work._comp_row computes completeness_pct = (non_null/total)*100; %IF %SYSEVALF(pct < threshold) %THEN check_status='FAIL' with %PUT WARNING, %ELSE check_status='PASS'; PROC APPEND BASE=work.completeness_&table DATA=work._comp_row FORCE; %END

DATA dq.completeness_&table inside check_completeness: SET work.completeness_&table to store final completeness results in DQ library; RUN; %PUT NOTE completeness check completed with column count; %MEND check_completeness

%MACRO check_validity(lib=, table=, rules_ds=) opening: Section 4 header (validates data against business rules from a rules dataset, reads each rule via SET, uses CALL EXECUTE to generate dynamic validation SQL, loops over rules to count violations, aggregates into quality scorecard); %PUT NOTE with lib, table, rules_ds parameters

DATA _NULL_ inside check_validity: SET &rules_ds END=last; LENGTH rule_sql $500 rule_id $10 rule_desc $200 target_column $32 rule_type $20; CALL EXECUTE generates PROC SQL NOPRINT for each rule — SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :violations_<rule_id> WHERE rule_condition; IF last THEN CALL SYMPUT('n_rules', _N_) to track total rule count; RUN

DATA work.validity_&table inside check_validity: initializes validity results table with LENGTH table_name $32 rule_id $10 rule_desc $200 target_column $32 rule_type $20 total_rows violation_count validity_pct threshold check_status $4 check_date; FORMAT validity_pct 6.2 check_date DATE9.; STOP; RUN

%DO r = 1 %TO &n_rules inside check_validity: loop over rules to collect violation counts; DATA work._rule_result from &rules_ds (FIRSTOBS=&r OBS=&r) retrieves violation_count via SYMGET; PROC SQL NOPRINT for rule_total row count; DATA work._rule_result computes validity_pct = ((total_rows - violation_count)/total_rows)*100, sets check_status PASS if >= threshold else FAIL; PROC APPEND BASE=work.validity_&table; %END
DATA dq.validity_&table inside check_validity: SET work.validity_&table to store validity results in DQ library; RUN; %PUT NOTE validity check completed with rule count; %MEND check_validity

%MACRO check_consistency(src1=, src2=, key=, compare_cols=) opening: Section 5 header (compares two data sources on shared key to find discrepancies using PROC SQL FULL JOIN, DATA step to classify match/mismatch/missing_left/missing_right, PROC MEANS for match rate); %PUT NOTE with src1, src2, key, compare_cols parameters; %LET n_compare = %SYSFUNC(COUNTW(&compare_cols))

PROC SQL inside check_consistency: CREATE TABLE work.consistency_raw with FULL JOIN — SELECT COALESCE(a.&key, b.&key), %DO loop over compare_cols to include left_&comp_col and right_&comp_col from both sources, CASE classification WHEN a.key NULL THEN MISSING_LEFT, WHEN b.key NULL THEN MISSING_RIGHT, WHEN all columns match via %DO loop THEN MATCH, ELSE MISMATCH; FROM &src1 a FULL JOIN &src2 b ON a.&key = b.&key; QUIT

DATA work.consistency_detail (KEEP=key match_status mismatch_columns check_date) and work.consistency_mismatches (WHERE=MISMATCH) inside check_consistency: LENGTH match_status $15 mismatch_columns $500; SET work.consistency_raw; check_date=TODAY(); %DO loop over compare_cols builds mismatch_columns via CATX for mismatched values; OUTPUT consistency_detail; IF MISMATCH THEN OUTPUT consistency_mismatches; RUN

PROC MEANS inside check_consistency: DATA=work.consistency_detail NOPRINT NWAY; CLASS match_status; OUTPUT OUT=work.consistency_stats (DROP=_TYPE_ _FREQ_) N=record_count; RUN — computes record counts by match status category

PROC SQL NOPRINT inside check_consistency: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN match_status='MATCH' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) * 100 FORMAT=6.2 INTO :match_rate TRIMMED FROM work.consistency_detail; QUIT — calculates overall match rate percentage

DATA dq.consistency_%SCAN(&src1,2,.)_%SCAN(&src2,2,.) inside check_consistency: SET work.consistency_detail; match_rate = &match_rate to add computed match rate; RUN; %PUT NOTE consistency check complete with match_rate percentage; %MEND check_consistency

%MACRO check_timeliness(lib=, table=, date_col=, max_lag_days=) opening: Section 6 header (checks data freshness by computing lag between most recent record date and current date, uses PROC SQL for max date, DATA step with %SYSFUNC for lag computation, flags stale data when lag exceeds max_lag_days); %PUT NOTE with lib, table, date_col, max_lag_days parameters

PROC SQL NOPRINT inside check_timeliness: SELECT MAX(&date_col) FORMAT=DATE9. INTO :max_date TRIMMED FROM &lib..&table; SELECT COUNT(*) INTO :table_rows TRIMMED; QUIT — gets most recent date value and total row count

DATA dq.timeliness_&table inside check_timeliness: LENGTH table_name $32 date_column $32 max_date current_date lag_days max_allowed_lag freshness_status $10 check_date total_rows; FORMAT dates DATE9.; assigns table_name, date_column, max_date via INPUT, current_date=TODAY(), max_allowed_lag=max_lag_days, total_rows; lag_days = INTCK('DAY', max_date, current_date); %IF %SYSEVALF(lag > max_lag_days) %THEN freshness_status='STALE' with %PUT WARNING, %ELSE freshness_status='CURRENT'; OUTPUT; RUN; %PUT NOTE timeliness check completed with max_date; %MEND check_timeliness

%MACRO dq_scorecard opening: Section 7 header (compiles all quality check results into weighted composite score, PROC SQL aggregation, DATA step for weighted composite, PROC REPORT for formatted scorecard, ODS Excel for distribution); %PUT NOTE building DQ Scorecard with run_date

PROC SQL inside dq_scorecard: CREATE TABLE work.score_completeness — aggregates completeness dimension scores via OUTER UNION CORR from dq.completeness_customers, dq.completeness_transactions, dq.completeness_products, dq.completeness_accounts, dq.completeness_orders; SELECT 'COMPLETENESS' AS dimension, AVG(completeness_pct) AS dimension_score, SUM failure count, COUNT total_checks, weight_completeness AS weight; QUIT

PROC SQL inside dq_scorecard: CREATE TABLE work.score_validity — aggregates validity dimension scores via OUTER UNION CORR from dq.validity_customers, dq.validity_transactions, dq.validity_products; SELECT 'VALIDITY' AS dimension, AVG(validity_pct) AS dimension_score, SUM failure count, COUNT total_checks, weight_validity AS weight; QUIT
DATA work.dq_dimensions inside dq_scorecard: SET work.score_completeness work.score_validity to combine completeness and validity dimension scores into single table; RUN

PROC SQL inside dq_scorecard: INSERT INTO work.dq_dimensions — adds consistency dimension scores from dq.consistency_customers_ext_customers; SELECT 'CONSISTENCY', match_rate AS dimension_score, CASE fail_count WHEN match_rate < consistency_threshold, 1 total_checks, weight_consistency AS weight; QUIT

DATA work.timeliness_scores inside dq_scorecard: SET dq.timeliness_customers, dq.timeliness_transactions, dq.timeliness_orders, dq.timeliness_accounts; LENGTH dimension $20; dimension='TIMELINESS'; IF freshness_status='CURRENT' THEN dimension_score=100 ELSE dimension_score = MAX(0, 100 - (lag_days-max_allowed_lag)*10); weight=weight_timeliness; fail_count=(freshness_status='STALE'); total_checks=1; RUN

PROC APPEND inside dq_scorecard: BASE=work.dq_dimensions DATA=work.timeliness_scores (KEEP=dimension dimension_score fail_count total_checks weight) FORCE — appends timeliness scores to combined dimensions table; RUN

DATA work.composite_score inside dq_scorecard: SET work.dq_dimensions END=last; RETAIN weighted_sum 0 weight_sum 0 total_fails 0 total_checks_all 0; accumulates weighted_sum + (dimension_score * weight), weight_sum, total_fails, total_checks_all; IF last THEN composite_score = weighted_sum/weight_sum, overall_status via IFC (EXCELLENT>=90, GOOD>=75, FAIR>=60, else POOR), scorecard_date=TODAY(); FORMAT scorecard_date DATE9. composite_score 6.2; OUTPUT; RUN

PROC REPORT inside dq_scorecard: DATA=work.dq_dimensions NOWD STYLE(HEADER)=[BACKGROUNDCOLOR=steelblue COLOR=white]; COLUMNS dimension dimension_score fail_count total_checks weight; DEFINE dimension / GROUP 'Quality Dimension'; DEFINE dimension_score / ANALYSIS MEAN 'Score (%)' FORMAT=6.2; DEFINE fail_count / SUM 'Failures'; DEFINE total_checks / SUM 'Total Checks'; DEFINE weight / DISPLAY FORMAT=4.2; COMPUTE AFTER with LINE for scorecard title and framework version; RUN

ODS Excel output inside dq_scorecard: ODS EXCEL FILE= dq_scorecard xlsx STYLE=SEASIDE OPTIONS(SHEET_NAME='DQ Scorecard', EMBEDDED_TITLES='YES'); PROC PRINT DATA=work.composite_score NOOBS LABEL with TITLE 'Enterprise Data Quality Composite Score' and run_date/version; PROC PRINT DATA=work.dq_dimensions NOOBS LABEL with TITLE 'Quality Dimension Detail'; ODS EXCEL CLOSE

DATA report.dq_scorecard_history inside dq_scorecard: SET report.dq_scorecard_history and work.composite_score to append current scorecard to history; RUN; %PUT NOTE DQ Scorecard generated successfully; %PUT NOTE report saved to /data/governance/reports/; %MEND dq_scorecard

Section 8 header (main program — execute all quality checks in sequence: profiling, completeness, validity, consistency, timeliness, scorecard); Phase 1 — DATA PROFILING: %profile_table(lib=source, table=customers), %profile_table(lib=source, table=transactions), %profile_table(lib=source, table=products), %profile_table(lib=source, table=accounts), %profile_table(lib=source, table=orders) — profiles 5 source tables

Phase 2 — COMPLETENESS CHECKS: %check_completeness for 5 tables — customers (customer_id name email phone address), transactions (txn_id customer_id amount txn_date status), products (product_id product_name category price), accounts (account_id customer_id account_type open_date balance), orders (order_id customer_id order_date total_amount ship_date)

Phase 3 — BUSINESS RULE VALIDATION: %check_validity for 3 tables with different rule sets — customers (rules_ds=meta.rules_customers), transactions (rules_ds=meta.rules_transactions), products (rules_ds=meta.rules_products)

Phase 4 — CROSS-SOURCE CONSISTENCY: %check_consistency for 2 comparisons — source.customers vs meta.ext_customers (key=customer_id, compare_cols=name email phone), source.accounts vs meta.ext_accounts (key=account_id, compare_cols=account_type balance status)

Phase 5 — DATA FRESHNESS CHECKS: %check_timeliness for 4 tables — customers (date_col=last_update_dt, max_lag_days=7), transactions (date_col=txn_date, max_lag_days=1), orders (date_col=order_date, max_lag_days=2), accounts (date_col=last_activity_dt, max_lag_days=3)
Phase 6 — DQ SCORECARD GENERATION: %dq_scorecard — compiles all quality dimension results into weighted composite score and generates formatted report with ODS Excel output

Phase 7 — FAILURE SUMMARY header and %PUT NOTE; DATA work.all_failures: LENGTH source_check $30 table_name $32 detail $200 check_status $4 check_date 8; FORMAT check_date DATE9.; SET dq.completeness_customers (IN=a WHERE FAIL), dq.completeness_transactions (IN=b), dq.completeness_products (IN=c), dq.completeness_accounts (IN=d), dq.completeness_orders (IN=e) — all filtered WHERE check_status='FAIL'; IF a-e THEN source_check='COMPLETENESS'; detail via CATX with column_name and completeness_pct; RUN

DATA work.validity_failures: LENGTH source_check $30 table_name $32 detail $200 check_status $4 check_date 8; FORMAT check_date DATE9.; SET dq.validity_customers (IN=a WHERE FAIL), dq.validity_transactions (IN=b), dq.validity_products (IN=c) — all filtered WHERE check_status='FAIL'; source_check='VALIDITY'; detail via CATX with rule_id and violation_count; RUN
PROC APPEND BASE=work.all_failures DATA=work.validity_failures FORCE — appends validity failure records to combined all_failures table; RUN

TITLE 'DATA GOVERNANCE FRAMEWORK — FAILURE SUMMARY'; TITLE2 with run_date and dq_version; TITLE3 with steward_name and domain_name; PROC PRINT DATA=work.all_failures NOOBS LABEL; VAR source_check table_name detail check_status check_date; LABEL source_check='Check Type', table_name='Table', detail='Details', check_status='Status', check_date='Date'; RUN — prints formatted failure summary report

TITLE; (clear titles); final program status log: %PUT NOTE banners — Data Governance Framework execution complete, run_date, framework version dq_version, steward_name, domain_name; trailing comment 'End of gsh_12_data_governance.sas'
